summary: Crisp audience tape captures a high‑energy ’83 Stanford Frost show with smoking Bird Song jam and a monster Estimated.
review: The recording is an excellent audience capture – bright highs, slight twang and a well‑defined soundstage despite occasional thinness.  The performance is upbeat and energetic, highlighted by a soaring Bird Song, tight Shakedown Street, fiery Eyes and a standout Estimated/Esau run.  Overall the show feels like a classic ’83 set that rewards both listening pleasure and Deadhead nostalgia.

ai_rating.rationale: Consistently high star ratings (mostly 4‑5) and enthusiastic comments about both the music and tape quality indicate a strong show with an excellent audience recording.

recording_quality.technical_notes: Bright highs, slight twang; overall crisp, well‑defined spectrum. A few reviewers note a thin spot in the mid‑range, but most describe it as one of the better sounding AUD tapes.

show_quality.setlist_flow: The set flows with high energy – upbeat tempos, tight transitions and extended jams (notably Estimated/Esau) that keep the momentum rolling.  Even the quieter moments feel purposeful, making for a cohesive ’83 concert experience.

band_member_comments.Jerry: Multiple reviewers note Jerry ‘rocked’ and delivered perfect tone throughout.
band_member_comments.Keys: A reviewer mentions “tinkly” keyboard work on They Love Each Other, indicating a pleasant keys texture.
band_member_comments.Drums: One comment highlights the “weirdness in the drummers” that year, noting an unusual but interesting rhythmic feel.
